description: Partitions every uncertainty source in CLEAR-ATS into one of four layers: scenario, epistemic (outer), aleatoric (inner), and structural-shock. Each entry names the config path and the computational treatment. Consumed by v6_uncertainty_rearchitecture/nested_mc.py and by the taxonomy tables.

scenario.meaning: Externally specified pathway family. Not a random draw. Selected by the analyst.
scenario.computational_treatment: Select one; run the entire MC pipeline conditional on that selection. Never blended into continuous priors.

epistemic_outer.meaning: Uncertainty about parameters that, once known, would not change year to year within a pathway world. Frozen per outer draw.

members.rationale: incomplete knowledge of 2024 low-carbon electricity share

epistemic_outer.computational_treatment: Single draw per outer-loop iteration. Frozen inside the inner loop.

aleatoric_inner.meaning: Irreducible year-to-year variation conditional on the outer epistemic world. Resampled each year of each inner realization.

members.rationale: weather / utilization modulation of annual ATS energy demand; v6 introduces this as Normal(1.0, 0.02) per year
short-horizon grid-mix realization around the epistemic clean fraction; v6 introduces this as Normal(1.0, 0.015) per year

aleatoric_inner.computational_treatment: Sampled per (outer, year) pair. Small, not the dominant source. See `nested_mc.py:_apply_aleatoric_realizations`.
aleatoric_inner.note: Aleatoric terms are deliberately small. v6 exposes them so that the architecture is correct (outer × inner); their numerical contribution should be expected to be a fraction of the epistemic band.

structural_shock.meaning: Discrete, labelled, low-probability regime breaks. Not probabilized into continuous priors.
structural_shock.computational_treatment: Run as separate named simulations. Compared visually against the baseline world; never merged into baseline MC quantile CSVs.
